# TORCH вЂ” Automated Gameplay QA Review

Seed **7**, **4000** ticks (~166 days) per persona. Generated by `torch-qa` driving the deterministic core through 9 play styles. Same seed в‡’ same review.

## Spectator

_Touches nothing вЂ” is the world alive and worth watching with hands off the controls? (В§28)_

| metric | value |
| --- | --- |
| treasury | 50000 в†’ 50000 cr (+0, ~1Г—) |
| actions | 0 over 0% of ticks |
| pacing | 2376 ticks pending В· longest idle 1049 ticks |
| campaign | The Station В· gate 0% В· 0 ascent(s) |
| gate reached | вЂ” |
| CEO level | 1 В· techs 0 |
| traffic | 166 flew, 130 arrived, 33 cut, 33 shortages |
| act-now alerts | 33 raised, 0 answered |
| standings (E/M/B/I) | 0 / 0 / 0 / 0 |
| market wall hits | 0 |

**Findings:**

- **[INFO]** _Pacing_ вЂ” Never advanced the campaign вЂ” this play style completed no operations (a cut, a commissioned ship, a founded station, or a delivered route), so it never drew the gate closer.
- **[GOOD]** _Watchability_ вЂ” Hands fully off, the world stayed alive: 166 convoys flew, 33 cut on the lanes, 33 shortages voiced. There is something to watch before there is something to do.
- **[NOTE]** _Alert feed_ вЂ” 33 act-now shortages were raised but none were acted on. The ExploitShortage verb needs matching cargo already on hand to exercise вЂ” there's no one-press path from the alert to the trade.
- **[GOOD]** _Pressure_ вЂ” Incoming raids were telegraphed 55 times across the run (В§13 forecasting) вЂ” threats arrive foreseen, not out of nowhere, and the pacing governor holds spikes apart.

## Privateer

_Cuts every convoy it can вЂ” climbs the retention spine and tracks the reputation cost (В§7b/В§0)._

| metric | value |
| --- | --- |
| treasury | 50000 в†’ 50000 cr (+0, ~1Г—) |
| actions | 174 over 4% of ticks |
| pacing | 3976 ticks pending В· longest idle 24 ticks |
| campaign | The Gate В· gate 100% В· 3 ascent(s) |
| gate reached | tick 913 (~38 days) |
| CEO level | 34 В· techs 0 |
| traffic | 166 flew, 0 arrived, 166 cut, 166 shortages |
| act-now alerts | 166 raised, 0 answered |
| standings (E/M/B/I) | -1000 / 1000 / 0 / 0 |
| market wall hits | 0 |

**Ascents:** The Region @ 73 в†’ Sol & the Cold War @ 313 в†’ The Gate @ 913

**Findings:**

- **[GOOD]** _Pacing_ вЂ” Opened the ring-gate at tick 913 (~38 days).
- **[NOTE]** _Economy_ вЂ” Active all run but treasury never moved вЂ” the loop found no work (e.g. a standing order idle below its margin). That idle state is the exception the feed should surface.
- **[GOOD]** _Agency_ вЂ” Acted on 4% of ticks (174 actions), but the dead time is fast-forwardable: the longest stretch with nothing pending was 24 ticks (~1 days, ~1 s at 24Г—). With time-compression + auto-pause-on-exception (В§28), the player compresses the quiet and is stopped only when an act-now alert fires.
- **[NOTE]** _Alert feed_ вЂ” 166 act-now shortages were raised but none were acted on. The ExploitShortage verb needs matching cargo already on hand to exercise вЂ” there's no one-press path from the alert to the trade.
- **[GOOD]** _Pressure_ вЂ” Incoming raids were telegraphed 55 times across the run (В§13 forecasting) вЂ” threats arrive foreseen, not out of nowhere, and the pacing governor holds spikes apart.
- **[NOTE]** _Reputation_ вЂ” Sustained raiding kept Earth pinned at Hostile (-1000). Standings now heal toward neutral when the raids stop (a recoverable dial, not a one-way cliff) вЂ” but a persona that raids every tick outruns the drift, so the price is still real.
- **[INFO]** _Reputation_ вЂ” Mars warmed to 1000 over the run.

## Expansionist

_Buys frontier colonies and holds them вЂ” does expansion pay off, and do administrative strain + the great-power coalition cap it? (Empire layer E1вЂ“E6)_

| metric | value |
| --- | --- |
| treasury | 38000 в†’ 174242 cr (+136242, ~4Г—) |
| actions | 173 over 4% of ticks |
| pacing | 3477 ticks pending В· longest idle 89 ticks |
| campaign | The Gate В· gate 100% В· 3 ascent(s) |
| gate reached | tick 2341 (~97 days) |
| CEO level | 13 В· techs 0 |
| traffic | 166 flew, 143 arrived, 21 cut, 21 shortages |
| act-now alerts | 21 raised, 0 answered |
| battles | 3 fought, 2 won |
| empire | 13 holding(s) at peak В· coalition alarm peak 1000 |
| standings (E/M/B/I) | -392 / -392 / 17 / 0 |
| market wall hits | 0 |

**Ascents:** The Region @ 0 в†’ Sol & the Cold War @ 421 в†’ The Gate @ 2341

**Findings:**

- **[GOOD]** _Pacing_ вЂ” Opened the ring-gate at tick 2341 (~97 days).
- **[GOOD]** _Economy_ вЂ” Turned a profit hands-on/over time: 38000 в†’ 174242 cr (+136242, ~4Г—).
- **[GOOD]** _Agency_ вЂ” Acted on 4% of ticks (173 actions), but the dead time is fast-forwardable: the longest stretch with nothing pending was 89 ticks (~3 days, ~3 s at 24Г—). With time-compression + auto-pause-on-exception (В§28), the player compresses the quiet and is stopped only when an act-now alert fires.
- **[NOTE]** _Alert feed_ вЂ” 21 act-now shortages were raised but none were acted on. The ExploitShortage verb needs matching cargo already on hand to exercise вЂ” there's no one-press path from the alert to the trade.
- **[GOOD]** _Pressure_ вЂ” Incoming raids were telegraphed 105 times across the run (В§13 forecasting) вЂ” threats arrive foreseen, not out of nowhere, and the pacing governor holds spikes apart.
- **[GOOD]** _Empire_ вЂ” Expansion-by-acquisition is reachable and exercised: grew to 13 holding(s) at peak (ending at 13). The В§0 spine now has a territorial dimension.
- **[GOOD]** _Empire В· overextension_ вЂ” Growth provoked the great-power coalition (alarm peaked 1000/1000), answered by the fleet. The political cap on reckless expansion bites вЂ” careful growth is a real decision.
- **[GOOD]** _Empire В· security_ вЂ” The trade empire drew piracy вЂ” 37 raids landed when escorts on station fell short of the holdings. A bigger empire needs a bigger navy (EP3): real, but counterable.
- **[GOOD]** _Empire В· enforcement_ вЂ” Souring the great powers brought customs enforcement вЂ” 11 inspection sweeps fined the empire's shipping (EP4). Trading in hostile space is taxed; repairing relations is the counter.

## Developer

_Acquires a couple of colonies, then grows them tall by developing вЂ” does the tall axis pay, and stay off the coalition's radar? (Phase C)_

| metric | value |
| --- | --- |
| treasury | 5000 в†’ 128590 cr (+123590, ~25Г—) |
| actions | 490 over 12% of ticks |
| pacing | 2088 ticks pending В· longest idle 1193 ticks |
| campaign | The Region В· gate 33% В· 1 ascent(s) |
| gate reached | вЂ” |
| CEO level | 2 В· techs 0 |
| traffic | 166 flew, 135 arrived, 29 cut, 29 shortages |
| act-now alerts | 29 raised, 0 answered |
| empire | 1 holding(s) at peak В· coalition alarm peak 90 |
| standings (E/M/B/I) | 0 / 0 / 0 / 0 |
| market wall hits | 0 |

**Ascents:** The Region @ 31

**Findings:**

- **[INFO]** _Pacing_ вЂ” Climbed to The Region (1 ascent(s)) but did not reach the gate within 4000 ticks (~166 days).
- **[CONCERN]** _Economy_ вЂ” Treasury ran away: 5000 в†’ 128590 cr (~25Г—) with no wealth-scaled sink. Repeated arbitrage compounds without bound, so trading stops being a decision and becomes a faucet.
- **[INFO]** _Agency_ вЂ” Issued 490 actions across 12% of ticks.
- **[NOTE]** _Alert feed_ вЂ” 29 act-now shortages were raised but none were acted on. The ExploitShortage verb needs matching cargo already on hand to exercise вЂ” there's no one-press path from the alert to the trade.
- **[GOOD]** _Pressure_ вЂ” Incoming raids were telegraphed 55 times across the run (В§13 forecasting) вЂ” threats arrive foreseen, not out of nowhere, and the pacing governor holds spikes apart.
- **[GOOD]** _Empire_ вЂ” Expansion-by-acquisition is reachable and exercised: grew to 1 holding(s) at peak (ending at 1). The В§0 spine now has a territorial dimension.
- **[GOOD]** _Empire В· development_ вЂ” Grew *tall*, not just wide: developed a holding to level 5/5, scaling its tribute + output вЂ” and (unlike wide expansion) drew no extra coalition alarm. A small, developed empire is a real alternative to a sprawling one (Phase C).
- **[NOTE]** _Empire В· overextension_ вЂ” Expansion stayed under the coalition threshold (alarm peaked 90/1000) вЂ” the great powers watched but never struck. A more aggressive expander would test the cap harder.
- **[GOOD]** _Empire В· security_ вЂ” The trade empire drew piracy вЂ” 44 raids landed when escorts on station fell short of the holdings. A bigger empire needs a bigger navy (EP3): real, but counterable.

## Engagement & fun assessment

_These are **structural proxies** for engagement, not a measure of subjective fun вЂ” a deterministic bot can flag aimlessness, dead air, flat stakes, a starved reward cadence, and dominant strategies, but it can't feel delight. Read the scores as "where is fun at risk?", not "how fun is it?"._

| persona | overall | Direction | Flow | Agency | Reward | Stakes | Variety |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Spectator | **22** | 0 | 74 | 0 | 0 | 22 | 49 |
| Arbitrageur | **28** | 0 | 100 | 0 | 0 | 30 | 49 |
| Logistician | **76** | 100 | 76 | 60 | 100 | 25 | 86 |
| Privateer | **90** | 100 | 100 | 60 | 100 | 100 | 79 |
| Warlord | **51** | 33 | 78 | 19 | 23 | 100 | 72 |
| Tycoon | **98** | 100 | 100 | 100 | 100 | 100 | 86 |
| Expansionist | **87** | 100 | 98 | 60 | 100 | 74 | 92 |
| Responder | **90** | 100 | 99 | 100 | 100 | 40 | 92 |
| Developer | **38** | 33 | 71 | 19 | 23 | 22 | 66 |

**Per-style read (overall + weakest link):**

- **Spectator** в–€в–€в–€В·В·В·В·В·В·В· 22/100 вЂ” weakest is _Direction_ (0/100): reached The Station (0% to the gate)
- **Arbitrageur** в–€в–€в–€В·В·В·В·В·В·В· 28/100 вЂ” weakest is _Direction_ (0/100): reached The Station (0% to the gate)
- **Logistician** в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€В·В· 76/100 вЂ” weakest is _Stakes_ (25/100): treasury dip 1%, 0 ships lost, rep low 0, pressure peak 29
- **Privateer** в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€В· 90/100 вЂ” weakest is _Agency_ (60/100): advanced 3/3 tiers by its own operations; answered 0/166 act-now shortages
- **Warlord** в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€В·В·В·В· 51/100 вЂ” weakest is _Agency_ (19/100): advanced 1/3 tiers by its own operations; answered 0/29 act-now shortages
- **Tycoon** в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€ 98/100 вЂ” weakest is _Variety_ (86/100): 7 of 9 event kinds; 4 tier(s) of scope
- **Expansionist** в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€В· 87/100 вЂ” weakest is _Agency_ (60/100): advanced 3/3 tiers by its own operations; answered 0/21 act-now shortages
- **Responder** в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€в–€В· 90/100 вЂ” weakest is _Stakes_ (40/100): treasury dip 7%, 0 ships lost, rep low 0, pressure peak 29
- **Developer** в–€в–€в–€в–€В·В·В·В·В·В· 38/100 вЂ” weakest is _Agency_ (19/100): advanced 1/3 tiers by its own operations; answered 0/29 act-now shortages

**What the comparison says about fun:**

- **[GOOD]** _Fun В· breadth_ вЂ” Several distinct play styles are engaging (["Logistician", "Privateer", "Warlord", "Tycoon", "Expansionist", "Responder"] all score в‰Ґ50/100). Depth-of-decision survives the choice of approach вЂ” no single dominant strategy starves the others.
- **[NOTE]** _Fun В· weakest link_ вЂ” Across all play styles, **Agency** is the weakest engagement dimension (avg 46/100) вЂ” the experience's biggest fun gap to invest in next.
- **[GOOD]** _Fun В· strength_ вЂ” **Flow** is the strongest dimension (avg 88/100) вЂ” the experience leans on it well.
- **[GOOD]** _Fun В· watchability_ вЂ” Hands fully off, the world scores 61/100 on flow+variety вЂ” the measure of whether it's worth watching before you act (В§28).

## UI usability audit

_A **static** affordance audit of the shell's contract with the sim вЂ” the gdext binding (`#[func]`) and how `godot/*.gd` wires it. It can't see pixels (that's the GUT view tests and the manual render pass); it catches the affordance gaps that quietly hurt usability: calls that would break, capability the player can't reach, and platform fit._

| metric | value |
| --- | --- |
| bindings exposed | 258 |
| wired by the shell | 203 (78%) |
| keyboard bindings | 42 |
| pointer/touch | 10 pointer hit(s), native touch: true |
| controls legend | true |

**Findings:**

- **[GOOD]** _UI В· wiring_ вЂ” Every one of the shell's 203 sim calls resolves to a real binding вЂ” no phantom calls that would break at runtime (GDScript wouldn't catch them until that path runs).
- **[NOTE]** _UI В· coverage_ вЂ” The shell wires 78% of the 258 exposed bindings; 55 are never referenced (e.g. ["admin_load", "alert_threshold", "ally_count", "alpha", "blueprint_discover", "blueprint_known_count"]). Some are deliberately read-only or future, but a verb the shell never calls is a capability the player can't reach.
- **[GOOD]** _UI В· exceptionв†’verb_ вЂ” The act-now exception loop is pressable: the shell wires a one-press answer to shortages/contracts, so an alert resolves into a verb rather than a dead notification (В§0.4).
- **[GOOD]** _UI В· status visibility_ вЂ” The load-bearing state is on screen вЂ” treasury, the tier/destination, the alert feed, and the now-goal are all read by the shell (Nielsen #1, the В§0 three-horizon stack).
- **[GOOD]** _UI В· recognition_ вЂ” A controls legend is on screen вЂ” the keymap is recognised, not recalled (Nielsen #6).
- **[NOTE]** _UI В· platform fit_ вЂ” 42 keyboard bindings, with native touch handling. Keep the touch surface first-class for the Android target (В§33).

## Design review вЂ” cross-cutting

What the comparison of play styles reveals about the design as it stands:

- **[GOOD]** _Retention spine_ вЂ” The spine listens to more than raiding: ["Logistician", "Warlord", "Tycoon", "Expansionist", "Responder", "Developer"] climbed without cutting a single convoy (commissions, founded stations, and delivered routes now count as operations). Pure manual teleport-trade still doesn't climb вЂ” by design, it's the degenerate verb.
- **[GOOD]** _Combat_ вЂ” Combat is reachable from the live loop: 6 fleet engagements fought (3 held the field) via Sim::engage_raiders, with losses applied to the fleet and a BattleResolved alert voiced вЂ” the В§7/В§9 resolver is in play, not just demo_duel.
- **[GOOD]** _Economy_ вЂ” Hand-trading no longer dominates the route: a brokerage fee prices the instant verb's free liquidity (113888 cr by hand vs 107159 cr routed), and routing now also climbs the spine вЂ” so the two are complementary, not strictly ordered.
- **[GOOD]** _Economy_ вЂ” Wealth-scaled overhead bounds the faucet: the Arbitrageur settled at ~2Г— (в‰€113888 cr) instead of compounding without limit вЂ” accumulation now hits a sustainable equilibrium where overhead meets income.
- **[GOOD]** _Logistics_ вЂ” The standing-order layer is a *table* now (Sim::routes): many routes run concurrently against a shared freighter pool, each with its own params and idle/in-transit exception вЂ” the spreadsheet-sim master-table the influence model wants, not a single Option.
